metadata.name: SBP Internet Banking Framework
metadata.description: The State Bank of Pakistan Regulations for the Security of Internet Banking, issued by the Payment Systems Department, provides a mandatory security framework for all banks in Pakistan offering internet banking services.
metadata.source_organization: State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) - Payment Systems Department
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SBP Regulations for the Security of Internet Banking is a mandatory regulatory framework issued by the State Bank of Pakistan for all banks in Pakistan offering internet banking services. Compliance is monitored through SBP supervision and reporting requirements.
metadata.framework_purpose: To help banks in Pakistan develop a formal Internet Banking Security Framework containing administrative, technical and physical safeguards based on best international practices, protecting against financial and reputational risks arising from IT security breaches.
metadata.framework_scope: All banks in Pakistan providing financial and/or non-financial transactions through internet, irrespective of software tool used by the bank and access devices used by its customers.
framework_objectives: Ensure security and integrity of data and systems so that customers' information has not been modified and systems are free from unauthorized access
Ensure confidentiality of customers' data in storage, during processing and in transit
Ensure reliability and availability of Internet Banking systems to provide prompt access to registered users and maintain operational effectiveness
Ensure accountability by designing SOPs, policies and controls to ensure traceability of all transactions
Adopt a proactive approach to detect unauthorized access and identify potential fraudulent transactions
metadata.target_audience: Board of Directors, Senior Management, IT Security Teams, Digital Banking Teams, Fraud Prevention Teams, and Compliance Officers of all SBP-regulated banks offering internet banking
required_artifacts: Internet Banking Security Framework document (BOD approved)
Security Risk Assessment document (reviewed by BOD, at least annually)
Quarterly Security Breach Reports to Payment Systems Department (Annexure-I)
metadata.regulatory_authority: State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) - Payment Systems Department
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in regulatory penalties, restrictions on internet banking services, and enforcement actions as per SBP regulations.
metadata.adoption_approach: Risk-based implementation with mandatory compliance for all banks providing internet banking services in Pakistan.

sections: 2.2.3. Security Controls Implementation for Outsourced Functions
Annexure-I: Details of Established Security Breaches in IB

controls.title: Scope of the Regulations
controls.description: These regulations are applicable to all banks in Pakistan providing financial and/or non-financial transactions through internet irrespective of software tool used by the bank and access devices used by its customers.
controls.full_text: These regulations are applicable to all banks in Pakistan providing financial and/or non financial transactions through internet irrespective of software tool used by the bank and access devices used by its customers.
evidence_requirements: Confirmation that bank offers internet banking services
Documentation of all internet banking delivery channels and access devices in use

controls.title: Internet Banking Security Framework
controls.description: Bank shall develop, implement and regularly review an Internet Banking Security Framework based on defined security objectives. The framework must be approved by BODs, clearly define roles and responsibilities, and include Security Risk Assessment, Security Controls Implementation and Security Controls Monitoring.
controls.full_text: Bank shall develop, implement and regularly review Internet Banking Security Framework based on the following key security objectives: a) Security and integrity of data and systems, to ensure that customers' information has not been modified and systems are free from unauthorized access; b) Confidentiality of customers' data in storage, during processing and in transit; c) Reliability and availability of Internet Banking systems to provide prompt access to systems for registered users and maintaining operational effectiveness; d) Accountability by designing SOPs, policies and controls to ensure traceability of all transactions; e) Proactive approach to detect unauthorized access and identification of potential fraudulent transactions. While developing the Internet Banking Security Framework the bank should take into account the complexity of systems, applications and products/services offered while at the same time ensuring the ease of usage and customers' convenience. Further the framework should clearly define the roles and responsibilities of Board of Directors (BODs), senior management and employees with regard to its approval, development and implementation. This Framework and any reviews thereafter should be duly approved by the BODs. The Internet Banking Security Framework shall include the following components: Security Risk Assessment, Implementation of Security Controls and Monitoring of Security Controls.
evidence_requirements: Board of Directors approved Internet Banking Security Framework document
Framework review records with BOD approval
Defined roles and responsibilities for BODs, senior management and employees
Documentation covering Security Risk Assessment, Security Controls Implementation and Security Controls Monitoring components

controls.title: Security Objective: Security and Integrity of Data and Systems
controls.description: The Internet Banking Security Framework shall ensure security and integrity of data and systems, ensuring that customers' information has not been modified and systems are free from unauthorized access.
controls.full_text: Security and integrity of data and systems, to ensure that customers' information has not been modified and systems are free from unauthorized access.
evidence_requirements: Data integrity controls documentation
Evidence that systems are protected from unauthorized access
Integrity monitoring or verification mechanisms

controls.title: Security Objective: Confidentiality of Customers' Data
controls.description: The Internet Banking Security Framework shall ensure confidentiality of customers' data in storage, during processing and in transit.
controls.full_text: Confidentiality of customers' data in storage, during processing and in transit.
evidence_requirements: Data confidentiality controls for data at rest (storage)
Data confidentiality controls for data in processing
Data confidentiality controls for data in transit

controls.title: Security Objective: Reliability and Availability of Internet Banking Systems
controls.description: The Internet Banking Security Framework shall ensure reliability and availability of Internet Banking systems to provide prompt access to systems for registered users and maintain operational effectiveness.
controls.full_text: Reliability and availability of Internet Banking systems to provide prompt access to systems for registered users and maintaining operational effectiveness.
evidence_requirements: System availability targets and monitoring evidence
Operational effectiveness measures and reports

controls.title: Security Objective: Accountability and Traceability
controls.description: The Internet Banking Security Framework shall ensure accountability by designing SOPs, policies and controls to ensure traceability of all transactions.
controls.full_text: Accountability by designing SOPs, policies and controls to ensure traceability of all transactions.
evidence_requirements: SOPs and policies for transaction traceability
Transaction traceability controls implementation evidence

controls.title: Security Objective: Proactive Fraud Detection
controls.description: The Internet Banking Security Framework shall adopt a proactive approach to detect unauthorized access and identify potential fraudulent transactions.
controls.full_text: Proactive approach to detect unauthorized access and identification of potential fraudulent transactions.
evidence_requirements: Fraud detection mechanisms and tools documentation
Unauthorized access detection controls evidence

controls.title: Security Risk Assessment
controls.description: The bank shall conduct and document a formal Security Risk Assessment for Internet Banking to identify, estimate and prioritize risks. BODs shall review the risk assessment and any subsequent reviews. The assessment shall be reviewed at least annually and immediately after a major security breach, significant infrastructure change, or introduction of a new product or service.
controls.full_text: The bank shall conduct and document a formal Security Risk Assessment for Internet Banking with a view of identifying, estimating and prioritizing risks to which its operations are exposed due to Internet Banking. The BODs should review the risk assessment document and any reviews conducted thereafter. The Security Risk Assessment should be reviewed at least once a year; however, in case of a major security breach, significant changes to the infrastructure and introduction of a new product or service, an immediate review of risk assessment should be carried out. Further, in case of a major security breach, risk assessment review should include a detailed analysis of the factors that cause such security breaches.
evidence_requirements: Formal Security Risk Assessment document
BOD review records of risk assessment
Evidence of at least annual review cycle
Ad-hoc review records triggered by security breaches, infrastructure changes or new product/service introductions
Root cause analysis in risk assessment reviews following major security breaches

controls.title: Business and Technology Environment Description
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall include a current and detailed description of the bank's business and technology environment and existing security measures, including identification of location, systems and methods for maintaining customers' information.
controls.full_text: A current and detailed description of bank's business and technology environment and existing security measures in place including identification of location, systems and methods for maintaining customers' information.
evidence_requirements: Documentation of bank's business and technology environment
Inventory of existing security measures
Identified locations, systems and methods for maintaining customers' information

controls.title: Identification of Information and Information Systems to be Protected
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall include identification of information and the information systems to be protected.
controls.full_text: An identification of information and the information systems to be protected.
evidence_requirements: Inventory of information assets to be protected
Inventory of information systems to be protected

controls.title: Classification and Ranking of Sensitive Systems and Data
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall include classification and ranking (high, medium, low) of sensitive systems, payment data and applications in order of importance based on assessment of threats and vulnerabilities.
controls.full_text: Classification and ranking (high, medium, low) of the sensitive systems, payment data and applications in order of their importance and based on the assessment of threats and vulnerabilities.
evidence_requirements: Classification and ranking records (high/medium/low) for sensitive systems, payment data and applications
Threat and vulnerability basis for the classification

controls.title: Assessment of Threats and Vulnerabilities
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall include assessment of potential threats and vulnerabilities to the security and integrity of customers' information, payments data, IT systems and applications.
controls.full_text: Assessment of potential threats and vulnerabilities to security and integrity of customers' information, payments data, IT systems and applications.
evidence_requirements: Threat assessment documentation covering customers' information, payments data, IT systems and applications
Vulnerability assessment documentation covering customers' information, payments data, IT systems and applications

controls.title: Assessment of Identity Theft and Identity Fraud Risks
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall include assessment of risks related to identity theft and identity fraud.
controls.full_text: Assessment of risks related to identity theft and identity fraud.
evidence_requirements: Identity theft risk assessment documentation
Identity fraud risk assessment documentation

controls.title: Evaluation of Existing Security Controls Effectiveness
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall include an evaluation of existing Security Controls' effectiveness against each identified threat and vulnerability.
controls.full_text: An evaluation of existing Security Controls' effectiveness against each threat and vulnerability.
evidence_requirements: Security controls effectiveness evaluation records mapped against each identified threat and vulnerability

controls.title: Service Providers Security and Contractual Responsibilities
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall address the security and contractual responsibilities of Service Providers (SPs), including customers who have access to the bank's systems and data.
controls.full_text: The security and contractual responsibilities of Service Providers (SPs), including customers who have access to the bank's systems and data.
evidence_requirements: Documentation of security and contractual responsibilities of all Service Providers
Assessment of access rights of customers who access bank's systems and data

controls.title: Outsourcing Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risks including Compliance, Concentration, Operational, Country and Legal should be assessed by the banks before entering and while managing Internet Banking outsourcing arrangements with Service Providers.
controls.full_text: Risks like Compliance, Concentration, Operational, Country and Legal should be assessed by the banks before entering and while managing Internet Banking outsourcing arrangements with the SPs.
evidence_requirements: Outsourcing risk assessment covering Compliance, Concentration, Operational, Country and Legal risks
Pre-engagement risk assessments for Service Providers
Ongoing risk management records for existing outsourcing arrangements

controls.title: Legal Environment Risk Assessment
controls.description: The Security Risk Assessment shall address risks related to the legal environment and the bank's responsibilities under Section 32, Section 41, Section 43, Section 70 and other relevant provisions of the Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfers Act 2007.
controls.full_text: Risk Assessment related to legal environment and bank's responsibilities under Section 32 (Availability of Documentation and Proof), section 41 (Burden of Proof), Section 43 (Liability of banks/Authorized Parties), section 70 (Secrecy and Privacy) and other relevant provisions of the Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfers Act 2007.
evidence_requirements: Legal environment risk assessment referencing Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfers Act 2007
Assessment of compliance with Section 32 (Availability of Documentation and Proof)
Assessment of compliance with Section 41 (Burden of Proof)
Assessment of compliance with Section 43 (Liability of banks/Authorized Parties)
Assessment of compliance with Section 70 (Secrecy and Privacy)

controls.title: Security Controls Implementation
controls.description: The bank shall ensure appropriate security arrangements and security controls to protect IT assets are in place, developed based on the Security Risk Assessment and commensurate with risk levels. The bank shall define minimum baseline Security Controls covering Access Controls, Network Access Controls, Operating System Access Controls, Application Access and Remote Access Controls.
controls.full_text: The bank shall ensure that appropriate security arrangements and security controls to protect IT assets (such as systems, applications, networks, data, and information and communication systems) are in place. Bank shall develop a set of controls based on the Security Risk Assessment document, commensurate with the risk levels to meet the control objectives. Bank shall define its set of minimum baseline Security Controls that include Access Controls (Access Rights Management, Electronic Authentication etc), Network Access Controls, Operating System Access Controls, Application Access and Remote Access Controls.
evidence_requirements: Minimum baseline Security Controls definition document
Security controls mapped to Security Risk Assessment findings and risk levels
Evidence of Access Controls (Access Rights Management, Electronic Authentication)
Evidence of Network Access Controls
Evidence of Operating System Access Controls
Evidence of Application Access Controls
Evidence of Remote Access Controls

controls.title: Authentication Controls
controls.description: Minimum authentication controls to be implemented by banks for Internet Banking customers, covering registration/enrollment, two-factor authentication, layered security for high-value transactions, account lockout and session controls, and periodic authentication risk assessment.
controls.full_text: Minimum Security Controls to be implemented by banks should include the following aspects under Authentication Controls.
evidence_requirements: Authentication controls policy and implementation documentation

controls.title: Customer Registration and Enrollment
controls.description: Registration/enrollment for Internet Banking customers should be done prior to offering Internet Banking products and services after due verification through appropriate means.
controls.full_text: Registration/enrollment for Internet Banking customers should be done prior to offering Internet Banking products and services after due verification through appropriate means.
evidence_requirements: Customer registration/enrollment process documentation
Evidence of identity verification procedures applied prior to granting Internet Banking access

controls.title: Two Factor Authentication (2FA)
controls.description: In order to authenticate customers who use Internet Banking products and services the bank shall implement at least Two Factor Authentication (2FA) such as Passwords (1st factor) and One time tokens, Dongles etc (2nd factor).
controls.full_text: In order to authenticate customers who use Internet Banking products and services the bank shall implement at least Two Factor Authentication (2FA) such as Passwords (1 factor) and One time tokens, Dongles etc (2nd factor).
evidence_requirements: Two Factor Authentication (2FA) implementation evidence
Documentation of 1st factor (e.g. passwords) and 2nd factor (e.g. OTP, Dongles) mechanisms deployed

controls.title: Layered Security for High Value Transactions
controls.description: Bank shall implement additional layered security programs for high value transactions processed through Internet Banking.
controls.full_text: Bank shall implement additional layered security programs for high value transactions processed through Internet Banking.
evidence_requirements: Layered security controls configuration for high-value transactions
Definition of thresholds that trigger additional security layers for high-value transactions

controls.title: Account Lockout, Session Timeout and Re-authentication Controls
controls.description: Authentication controls should take into account failed log-in attempts, frequency of password changes, session timeouts and re-authentication of customers based on predefined criteria.
controls.full_text: Authentication controls should also take into account failed log-in attempts, frequency of password changes, session time outs and re-authentication of customers based on predefined criteria.
evidence_requirements: Account lockout policy and configuration for failed log-in attempts
Password change frequency policy and configuration
Session timeout configuration
Re-authentication criteria and implementation evidence

controls.title: Periodic Risk Assessment of Authentication Controls
controls.description: Bank shall conduct periodic risk assessment of authentication controls to identify threats and vulnerabilities based on changes in applications' functionality, changes in internal and external environment, changes in customers' preferences and actual security breaches.
controls.full_text: Bank shall conduct periodic risk assessment of authentication controls to identify threats and vulnerabilities based on changes in applications' functionality, threats due to changes in internal and external environment, changes in customers' preferences and actual security breaches.
evidence_requirements: Periodic risk assessment records for authentication controls
Evidence of reviews triggered by changes in application functionality
Evidence of reviews triggered by changes in internal and external environment
Evidence of reviews triggered by changes in customers' preferences
Evidence of reviews triggered by actual security breaches

controls.title: Security Controls for In-house Functions
controls.description: Security controls applicable to bank employees who are users of Internet Banking related systems, covering access rights management, OS controls, remote access, physical access, network security, firewalls, IDS/IPS, identity theft prevention, encryption, traceability and training.
controls.full_text: The following controls shall apply on bank employees who are users of Internet Banking related systems.
evidence_requirements: Overall in-house security controls policy covering all sub-requirements under 2.2.2

controls.title: Access Rights Management
controls.description: Users' access rights should be appropriate and commensurate with their job functions, periodically reviewed per risk ranking from the Security Risk Assessment, and changes applied only after due authorization, ensuring proper implementation of Least Privilege Principle.
controls.full_text: Access Rights Management: Users' access rights should be appropriate and commensurate with their job functions and should be periodically reviewed keeping in view the risk ranking of the systems, data and applications as outlined in Security Risk Assessment document. Changes in Access Rights should be based on personal or systems change and should only be applied after due authorization while ensuring proper implementation of 'Least Privilege Principle'.
evidence_requirements: Access rights policy implementing Least Privilege Principle
Periodic access rights review records (per risk ranking of systems, data and applications from Security Risk Assessment)
Access rights change authorization records confirming changes are based on personal or systems change and duly authorized

controls.title: Operating Systems Controls
controls.description: Necessary Operating Systems' controls should be implemented to ensure access is physically and logically secured, with privileged access restricted, regularly monitored and periodically audited.
controls.full_text: Operating Systems Controls: Necessary Operating Systems' controls should be implemented to ensure that access is physically and logically secured by ensuring that privileged access is restricted, regularly monitored and periodically audited.
evidence_requirements: Operating system access controls configuration showing physical and logical security
Privileged access restriction evidence
Privileged access monitoring records
Periodic privileged access audit records

controls.title: Remote Access
controls.description: Remote access to high risk IT assets shall only be granted after management's written approval, shall be subject to regular audits, and shall be based on strong authentication and encryption to secure communications.
controls.full_text: Remote Access: Remote access to high risk IT assets shall only be granted after management's approval in writing and should be subject to regular audits. Remote access shall also be based on strong authentication and encryption to secure communications.
evidence_requirements: Written management approvals for remote access to high risk IT assets
Regular audit records for remote access
Strong authentication implementation for remote access
Encryption implementation for remote access communications

controls.title: Physical Access
controls.description: Banks shall ensure that physical access to different systems, segments and data sites is restricted, regularly monitored and duly logged.
controls.full_text: Physical Access: Banks shall ensure that physical access to different systems, segments and data sites is restricted, regularly monitored and duly logged.
evidence_requirements: Physical access restriction controls for systems, segments and data sites
Physical access monitoring records
Physical access logs

controls.title: IT Network Security
controls.description: IT networks shall be secured through the use of multiple layers of controls.
controls.full_text: IT Network Security: IT networks shall be secured through the use of multiple layers of controls.
evidence_requirements: IT network security architecture documentation showing multiple layers of controls

controls.title: Firewalls
controls.description: Firewalls shall be deployed between different security domains to control network traffic. Firewall selection and deployment policy should be devised according to the characteristics of the network including traffic volume and risk classification of IT assets.
controls.full_text: Firewalls: Firewalls shall be deployed between different security domains to control network traffic. Firewalls selection and deployment policy should be devised according to the characteristics of network (i.e. traffic volume, and risk classification of IT assets).
evidence_requirements: Firewall deployment architecture showing placement between different security domains
Firewall selection and deployment policy based on traffic volume and risk classification of IT assets

controls.title: IDS/IPS Deployment
controls.description: Network Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) and Intrusion Prevention Systems (IPS) should be deployed between different security domains as per their risk classification.
controls.full_text: IDS/IPS: Network Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) and Intrusion Prevention Systems (IPS) should be deployed between different security domains as per their risk classification.
evidence_requirements: IDS/IPS deployment architecture showing placement between security domains per risk classification

controls.title: Identity Theft Prevention Program
controls.description: Bank shall develop and implement a proactive Identity Theft Prevention Program which includes procedures for identification of information to be protected, threats due to thefts and frauds, and methods for responding appropriately to identified threats.
controls.full_text: Identity Theft Prevention: Bank shall develop and implement a proactive Identity Theft Prevention Program which includes procedures for identification of information to be protected, and threats due to thefts and frauds as well as methods for responding appropriately to identified threats.
evidence_requirements: Identity Theft Prevention Program documentation
Procedures for identification of information to be protected from identity theft
Threat identification procedures covering thefts and frauds
Response methods for identified identity theft threats

controls.title: Encryption
controls.description: Access, storage and data communication shall be encrypted using reliable encryption methods to strengthen the security of communications and sensitive payment data.
controls.full_text: Encryption: Access, storage and data communication shall be encrypted using reliable encryption methods to strengthen the security of communications and sensitive payment data.
evidence_requirements: Encryption implementation evidence for access channels
Encryption implementation evidence for data storage
Encryption implementation evidence for data communications
Reliable encryption methods documentation for securing sensitive payment data

controls.title: Traceability
controls.description: Bank shall maintain the traceability of transactions executed to discharge their obligations under the relevant sections of the Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfers Act 2007.
controls.full_text: Traceability: Bank shall maintain the traceability of transactions executed to discharge their obligations under the relevant sections of Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfers Act 2007.
evidence_requirements: Transaction traceability logs and audit trails
Evidence of compliance with relevant sections of Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfers Act 2007

controls.title: Employee Training
controls.description: Relevant bank employees shall have appropriate knowledge and background to perform their tasks. Regular trainings should be arranged to keep employees aware of security risks, security controls and monitoring mechanisms. Employees should be regularly updated about changes in internal policies and procedures to ensure operational effectiveness.
controls.full_text: Training: Relevant employees of the bank shall have appropriate knowledge and background to perform their tasks. Regular trainings should be arranged to keep employees aware of the security risks, security controls and security control monitoring mechanisms. Employees should be regularly updated about the changes in internal policies and procedures to ensure operational effectiveness.
evidence_requirements: Employee training records covering security risks, security controls and monitoring mechanisms
Training schedule and curriculum documentation
Records of employee updates on changes to internal policies and procedures

controls.title: Security Controls Implementation for Outsourced Functions
controls.description: Service Providers of outsourced Internet Banking functions are contractually bound to implement these regulations. Outsourcing does not relieve the bank of its compliance responsibility. Banks shall ensure all controls in section 2.2.2 plus the additional outsourcing-specific controls are followed.
controls.full_text: Service Providers of outsourced functions relating to Internet Banking are contractually bounded to implement these regulations. However, outsourcing does not relieve the bank from its responsibility of complying with these regulations. Further bank shall ensure that in addition to security controls mentioned in section 2.2.2, the following security controls for outsourced functions are also followed.
evidence_requirements: Service Provider contracts confirming contractual obligation to implement SBP regulations
Bank's own compliance records confirming outsourcing does not reduce bank's regulatory responsibility

controls.title: Non-Disclosure and Right to Audit in Service Provider Contracts
controls.description: Contracts with Service Providers should include Non-Disclosure provisions to ensure confidentiality and security of bank and customers' information, and shall grant the bank as well as SBP the right to audit Service Providers' security controls.
controls.full_text: Contracts with Service Providers should be equipped with provision relating to Non Disclosure to ensure the confidentiality and security of bank and customers' information and to bank's as well as SBP's right to audit Service Providers' security controls as per the requirements set forth in these regulations.
evidence_requirements: Service Provider contracts containing Non-Disclosure provisions
Contract clauses granting the bank the right to audit Service Providers' security controls
Contract clauses granting SBP the right to audit Service Providers' security controls

controls.title: Customer Information Protection by Service Providers
controls.description: Appropriate measures to avoid any loss, theft or leakage of customers' information shall be developed and implemented by the Service Providers.
controls.full_text: Appropriate measures to avoid any loss, theft or leakage of customers' information are developed and implemented by the Service Providers.
evidence_requirements: Service Provider implemented measures to prevent loss, theft or leakage of customers' information
Evidence of bank's verification that such measures are in place at Service Providers

controls.title: Controlled and Monitored Service Provider Access to Customer Information
controls.description: Service Providers' access to customer information shall be request-based, strictly monitored, logged and periodically audited. Customer information shall not be transferred to unauthorized storage or access medium.
controls.full_text: Service Providers' access to customer information is based on the request from the bank or the concerned customer and should be strictly monitored, logged and periodically audited. Further, customer information shall not be transferred to unauthorized storage or access medium.
evidence_requirements: Request-based access process documentation for Service Provider access to customer information
Service Provider access monitoring records
Service Provider access logs
Periodic audit records for Service Provider customer information access
Controls ensuring customer information is not transferred to unauthorized storage or access medium

controls.title: Service Provider Disaster Recovery Requirements
controls.description: Service Providers shall have a Disaster Recovery plan aligned with bank's business requirements, different types of DR Sites (Cold/Warm/Hot/Satellite), and IT assets life cycle management documentation.
controls.full_text: Service Providers shall also have: i. A Disaster Recovery (DR) plan and its alignment with bank's business requirements. ii. Different types of Disaster Recovery (DR) Sites: Cold / Warm / Hot / Satellite. iii. Documentation related to IT assets life cycle management.
evidence_requirements: Service Provider Disaster Recovery (DR) plan
Evidence of DR plan alignment with bank's business requirements
Documentation of Service Provider DR Site types (Cold/Warm/Hot/Satellite)
Service Provider IT assets life cycle management documentation

controls.title: Service Provider Disaster Recovery Plan
controls.description: Service Providers shall have a Disaster Recovery (DR) plan aligned with the bank's business requirements.
controls.full_text: A Disaster Recovery (DR) plan and its alignment with bank's business requirements.
evidence_requirements: Service Provider Disaster Recovery (DR) plan document
Evidence of alignment of DR plan with bank's business requirements

controls.title: Service Provider Disaster Recovery Sites
controls.description: Service Providers shall have different types of Disaster Recovery (DR) Sites: Cold / Warm / Hot / Satellite.
controls.full_text: Different types of Disaster Recovery (DR) Sites: Cold / Warm / Hot / Satellite.
evidence_requirements: Documentation of Service Provider DR Site types (Cold/Warm/Hot/Satellite)
Evidence of operational DR sites at Service Provider

controls.title: Service Provider IT Assets Life Cycle Management
controls.description: Service Providers shall have documentation related to IT assets life cycle management.
controls.full_text: Documentation related to IT assets life cycle management.
evidence_requirements: Service Provider IT assets life cycle management documentation

controls.title: Security Controls Monitoring
controls.description: Bank shall develop and implement a formally approved mechanism for monitoring of Security Controls, including an analysis of the effectiveness of existing or proposed monitoring methods.
controls.full_text: Bank shall develop and implement a formally approved mechanism for the monitoring of Security Controls. An analysis of the effectiveness of existing or proposed Security Controls Monitoring methods shall be part of this monitoring mechanism.
evidence_requirements: Formally approved Security Controls Monitoring mechanism document
Effectiveness analysis of Security Controls Monitoring methods

controls.title: Network Activity Monitoring
controls.description: The monitoring mechanism shall cover monitoring of bank's network activity by collecting and analyzing host and network data related to security events, such as privileged access to sensitive operating systems, configuration changes, and access to critical applications.
controls.full_text: Monitoring of bank's network activity by collecting and analyzing the host and network data related to security events. Examples of security events include privileged access to sensitive operating systems, configuration changes, and access to critical applications etc.
evidence_requirements: Network activity monitoring records (host and network data collection and analysis)
Security event logs covering privileged access to sensitive OS, configuration changes and access to critical applications

controls.title: IDS/IPS Monitoring and Security Breach Response
controls.description: Methods for proactive monitoring of IDS/IPS and for responding to security breaches shall be detailed in the monitoring mechanism. A rapid response team shall be nominated and made responsible to respond immediately in case of a security breach.
controls.full_text: Methods for proactive monitoring of IDS/IPS and for responding to security breaches should be listed in detail in the monitoring mechanism. A rapid response team should be nominated and made responsible to respond immediately in case of a security breach.
evidence_requirements: Detailed IDS/IPS proactive monitoring methods documentation
Security breach response procedures
Nominated rapid response team with defined responsibilities for immediate security breach response

controls.title: Authentication Controls Monitoring and Reporting
controls.description: The monitoring and reporting mechanism for Authentication Controls shall be formally documented and approved by senior management and implemented accordingly.
controls.full_text: Monitoring and reporting mechanism of Authentication Controls should be formally documented and approved by the senior management and implemented accordingly.
evidence_requirements: Formally documented Authentication Controls monitoring and reporting mechanism
Senior management approval evidence for Authentication Controls monitoring mechanism

controls.title: System Restoration Procedures
controls.description: Procedures and time required for restoration of bank's systems shall be part of the Security Controls Monitoring process.
controls.full_text: Procedures and time required for restoration of bank's systems should be part of Security Controls Monitoring process.
evidence_requirements: Documented system restoration procedures
Defined and tested restoration timeframes

controls.title: Security Testing
controls.description: The monitoring mechanism shall include use of self-assessments, penetration testing, and independent security audits commensurate with the systems' complexity and risk exposures.
controls.full_text: Use of self-assessments, penetration testing, and independent security audits commensurate with the systems' complexity and risk exposures.
evidence_requirements: Self-assessment records
Penetration testing records
Independent security audit records
Evidence that testing frequency and scope is commensurate with system complexity and risk exposures

controls.title: Policy Violation and Unauthorized Configuration Change Identification
controls.description: The monitoring mechanism shall include identification and listing of bank's policy violations, unauthorized configuration changes and other conditions which can potentially increase the risk of security breaches.
controls.full_text: Identification and listing of bank's policy violations, unauthorized configuration changes and other conditions which can potentially increase the risk of security breaches.
evidence_requirements: Policy violation identification and listing records
Unauthorized configuration change identification and listing records
Records of other conditions identified as potentially increasing security breach risk

controls.title: Log and Audit Trail Monitoring and Retention
controls.description: Procedures shall ensure monitoring of logs and audit trails on a regular and predefined periodic basis. Internet Banking security logs and audit trails shall be retained for a period of ten years.
controls.full_text: Procedures to ensure the monitoring of logs and audit trails on regular and predefined periodic basis should be developed. The security logs and audit trials for IB should be retained for a period of ten years.
evidence_requirements: Log and audit trail monitoring procedures (regular and predefined periodic basis)
Evidence that Internet Banking security logs and audit trails are retained for ten years

controls.title: Customer Awareness
controls.description: A formal customer awareness program regarding Internet Banking threats and safeguards to minimize frauds and Identity Theft risks should be developed and implemented by the banks.
controls.full_text: A formal customer awareness program regarding Internet Banking threats and safeguards to minimize frauds and Identity Theft risks should be developed and implemented by the banks.
evidence_requirements: Formal Customer Awareness Program document

controls.title: Explanation of Liabilities, Roles and Responsibilities
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall include an explanation of liabilities, roles and responsibilities of the bank as well as its customers for using Internet Banking products and services offered by the bank.
controls.full_text: An explanation of liabilities, roles and responsibilities of bank as well as its customers for using Internet Banking products and services offered by the bank.
evidence_requirements: Published documentation explaining liabilities, roles and responsibilities of both bank and customers for Internet Banking use

controls.title: Disclosure Compliance - Section 30 of Payment Systems Act
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall ensure compliance with Disclosure requirements under Section 30 (Terms and Conditions of Transfers) of the Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfer Act 2007.
controls.full_text: Compliance of Disclosure requirements under Section 30 (Terms and Conditions of Transfers) of Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfer Act 2007.
evidence_requirements: Disclosure documentation compliant with Section 30 (Terms and Conditions of Transfers) of Payment Systems and Electronic Fund Transfer Act 2007

controls.title: Help Desk Contact Details
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall include contact details of the help desk that customers might need in case of any issues including loss of security credentials, frauds and identity theft.
controls.full_text: Contact details of help desk that customer might need in case of any issues including loss of security credentials or frauds and identity theft.
evidence_requirements: Published help desk contact details covering lost security credentials, fraud and identity theft issues

controls.title: Re-authentication Procedure
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall include the procedure for re-authentication of customers' Internet Banking.
controls.full_text: Procedure for re-authentication of customers Internet Banking.
evidence_requirements: Published customer-facing re-authentication procedure for Internet Banking

controls.title: Complaint Handling and Dispute Resolution
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall include the complaint handling process including dispute resolution mechanism related to Internet Banking.
controls.full_text: Complaint handling process including dispute resolution mechanism related to Internet Banking.
evidence_requirements: Complaint handling process documentation
Internet Banking dispute resolution mechanism documentation
Evidence that dispute resolution process is communicated to customers

controls.title: Annual Customer Guidelines
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall include regular issuance of guidelines to customers on a yearly basis for mitigating the risks associated with Internet Banking.
controls.full_text: Regular issuance of guidelines to customers on yearly basis for mitigating the risks associated with Internet Banking.
evidence_requirements: Records of annual guidelines issued to customers for mitigating Internet Banking risks

controls.title: Management Review of Customer Awareness Programs
controls.description: The customer awareness program shall be regularly reviewed and evaluated by the management.
controls.full_text: Regular review and evaluation of the customer awareness programs by the management.
evidence_requirements: Management review and evaluation records of customer awareness programs

controls.title: Reporting Requirements
controls.description: All established security breaches shall be reported to the Payment Systems Department, State Bank of Pakistan. Incident and analysis reports shall be furnished quarterly to PSD as per Annexure-I, including detailed impact on the institution's business, systems, applications and customers.
controls.full_text: All established security breaches should be reported to Payment Systems Department, State Bank of Pakistan. The incident and analysis reports of security breaches should be furnished on quarterly basis to PSD as per Annexure-I. Impact of security breach on institution's business, systems, applications and customers should also be submitted in detail.
evidence_requirements: Quarterly security breach incident and analysis reports submitted to SBP Payment Systems Department (Annexure-I format)
Detailed impact reports covering effects on institution's business, systems, applications and customers
Internal process for identifying and escalating established security breaches for regulatory reporting

controls.title: Regulatory Requirements
controls.description: These regulations are subject to all relevant SBP laws, rules and regulations including Guidelines on Outsourcing Arrangements, Guidelines on Information Technology Security, Guidelines on Information Systems Audits and System Switchover Planning, and Guidelines on Business Continuity Planning.
controls.full_text: These regulations are subject to all relevant laws, rules and regulations issued by SBP from time-to-time including but not limited to the following: a) Guidelines on the Outsourcing Arrangements (BPRD Circular No 9 dated July 13, 2007); b) Guidelines on the Information Technology Security (BSD Circular No 15 dated September 29, 2004); c) Information Systems: Guidelines on Audits and System Switchover Planning (BSD Circular No 8 dated December 12, 2005); d) Compliance of 'Guidelines on Business Continuity Planning' (BSD Circular No 13 dated September 04, 2004).
evidence_requirements: Compliance documentation for all relevant SBP regulations

controls.title: Compliance with Outsourcing Arrangements Guidelines
controls.description: These regulations are subject to the Guidelines on the Outsourcing Arrangements (BPRD Circular No 9 dated July 13, 2007).
controls.full_text: Guidelines on the Outsourcing Arrangements (BPRD Circular No 9 dated July 13, 2007).
evidence_requirements: Compliance documentation for BPRD Circular No 9 (July 13, 2007) - Guidelines on Outsourcing Arrangements

controls.title: Compliance with IT Security Guidelines
controls.description: These regulations are subject to the Guidelines on the Information Technology Security (BSD Circular No 15 dated September 29, 2004).
controls.full_text: Guidelines on the Information Technology Security (BSD Circular No 15 dated September 29, 2004).
evidence_requirements: Compliance documentation for BSD Circular No 15 (September 29, 2004) - Guidelines on Information Technology Security

controls.title: Compliance with IS Audits and System Switchover Planning Guidelines
controls.description: These regulations are subject to the Information Systems Guidelines on Audits and System Switchover Planning (BSD Circular No 8 dated December 12, 2005).
controls.full_text: Information Systems: Guidelines on Audits and System Switchover Planning (BSD Circular No 8 dated December 12, 2005).
evidence_requirements: Compliance documentation for BSD Circular No 8 (December 12, 2005) - Guidelines on Information Systems Audits and System Switchover Planning

controls.title: Compliance with Business Continuity Planning Guidelines
controls.description: These regulations are subject to the Guidelines on Business Continuity Planning (BSD Circular No 13 dated September 04, 2004).
controls.full_text: Compliance of 'Guidelines on Business Continuity Planning' (BSD Circular No 13 dated September 04, 2004).
evidence_requirements: Compliance documentation for BSD Circular No 13 (September 04, 2004) - Guidelines on Business Continuity Planning

controls.title: Details of Established Security Breaches in IB (Quarterly Report Template)
controls.description: Prescribed quarterly reporting format for banks to report established Internet Banking security breaches to the Payment Systems Department, SBP. Includes bank identification, submission details and structured breach information fields.
controls.full_text: Annexure-I: Details of Established Security Breaches in IB. Name of the bank, For the Quarter Ending, Submitted By (Name), Designation and Seal, Contact No. Table columns: Sr., Source of security breach discovery, Nature of security breach, Reasons for the occurrence of security breach (e.g. Breach of controls, Procedures were not followed, weaknesses in implemented security controls etc.), Impact of security breach (e.g. on banks business, systems, customers etc), Action(s) taken to rectify the Security Breaches, Remarks (further details, if any).
evidence_requirements: Completed Annexure-I quarterly reports submitted to PSD, SBP
Report fields: bank name, quarter ending date, submitter name, designation and seal, contact number
Breach detail fields: source of breach discovery, nature of breach, reasons for occurrence, impact on business/systems/customers, rectification actions taken, remarks
